data.title: KBRA assigns preliminary ratings to $272.8M non-prime RMBS from Angel Oak Mortgage Trust 2026-2 with 585 residential mortgages.
data.description: KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to ten classes of mortgage-backed certificates from Angel Oak Mortgage Trust 2026-2 (AOMT 2026-2), a non-prime RMBS transaction worth $272.8 million. The pool consists of 585 residential mortgages, with a significant proportion being loans underwritten using alternative income documentation. Among these loans, 52.1% are non-qualified mortgages (Non-QM) while the remaining 47.9% are exempt from the Ability-to- Repay/Qualified Mortgage (QM) rule.

data.title: VERSES filed Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for 3 and 9-months ended Dec. 31, 2025 with SEC. Vancouver-based cognitive computing company.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on February 20, 2026, VERSES AI Inc. (CBOE: VERS) (OTCQB: VRSSF), a leading cognitive computing company, released news that it submitted its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The report covers the financial performance for the three and nine months concluding on December 31, 2025.

data.title: KBRA assigns ratings to Aspire Mortgage Trust 2026-1 in a $391.3 million non-prime RMBS transaction with 752 residential mortgages comprising 99.2% FRMs and 0.8% hybrid ARMs.
data.description: In New York, KBRA has given preliminary ratings to eight classes of mortgage-backed notes from Aspire Mortgage Trust 2026-1 (SPIRE 2026-1), with a total transaction value of $391.3 million. The collateral consists of 752 residential mortgages, with 99.2% being fixed-rate mortgages (FRMs) and 0.8% being hybrid adjustable-rate mortgages (ARMs). These loans are categorized as Qualified Mortgages – Safe Harbor (APOR), with most falling under QM: Safe Harbor (A).

data.title: Tariff decision may lead to lower prices for consumers as some companies could delay price hikes.
data.description: Certain businesses may opt to limit price hikes, although it could be a while before the impact is felt.

data.title: DXP Enterprises, Inc. will announce Q4 and Fiscal 2025 earnings on Feb 25th post-market.
data.description: Houston-based DXP Enterprises, Inc., a prominent distributor of business to business products and services, is set to unveil its financial performance for the fourth quarter and Fiscal Year ending December 31, 2025. The company, trading on NASDAQ under the symbol DXPE, specializes in offering cost-effective solutions to MRO and OEM customers across various sectors. The earnings report is scheduled for release on February 25th post market closure. In addition to the press release, DXP will conduct a conference to discuss these results.

data.title: Chelsea FC partners with IFS for AI tech support on and off the field. Multi-year deal announced on Feb. 20, 2026.
data.description: Chelsea Football Club has recently entered into a multi-year global partnership with IFS, a prominent provider of Industrial AI software. This collaboration will involve leveraging IFS AI technology to improve performance both on and off the pitch. The partnership was announced on February 20, 2026, in London. The details of the agreement and the specific innovations to be implemented were not disclosed in the news update.

data.title: Study in Mobile, AL: Low-dose Ketamine aids Fentanyl users to switch to Buprenorphine with less severe withdrawal. 73,000 US deaths from overdoses last year, mostly from Fentanyl.
data.description: Last year, approximately 73,000 deaths in the United States were attributed to drug overdoses, with fentanyl, a synthetic opioid, being responsible for the majority of these fatalities. A recent study has discovered that Ketamine can alleviate fentanyl withdrawal symptoms and expedite the recovery process. Additionally, the study highlights the effectiveness of Buprenorphine, a medication commonly used for...

data.title: Bitcoin's potential $1 million push could be driven by institutions due to low market sentiment; Fear & Greed Index at 5.
data.description: Bitcoin, the well-known cryptocurrency, has the potential to reach a staggering $1 million if it manages to avoid dropping to zero. However, the market direction is now being primarily influenced by institutional investors rather than individual retail traders. Anthony Pompliano highlighted this shift in the latest episode of his podcast, attributing the current low sentiment towards Bitcoin to the escalating artificial intelligence competition. Pompliano mentioned that Bitcoin's Fear & Greed Index plunged to 5 as per data from Binance Research, marking one of the lowest levels recorded and indicating significant retail investor capitulation. This information was shared in the recent episode of the "From The Desk of Anthony Pompliano" podcast.

data.title: Supreme Court rules President can't impose tariffs under IEEPA, Wipfli advises businesses to evaluate exposure.
data.description: In Milwaukee on February 20, 2026, Wipfli, a prominent national accounting and advisory firm, is recommending companies to thoroughly assess their tariff risks in light of the recent ruling by the Supreme Court of the United States. The decision stated that President Trump does not possess the legal power to enforce tariffs. It is essential for businesses to carefully review their tariff implications in the current scenario.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures rise after Trump tariff ruling, S&P 500 surpasses 50-day line. Iran news and Nvidia earnings in focus.
data.description: The S&P 500 has reclaimed its 50-day moving average, showcasing resilience in a volatile market environment. Despite this positive development, the market continues to fluctuate unpredictably, presenting challenges for investors. Recent market activity, including the rebound in Dow Jones Futures following a ruling on Trump tariffs and upcoming events such as news from Iran and Nvidia earnings, are expected to have a significant impact on market trends.

data.title: FERC approves Blackstone Infrastructure's acquisition of TXNM Energy, deems it in public interest.
data.description: In Albuquerque, New Mexico on February 20, 2026, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) approved the acquisition of TXNM Energy (NYSE: TXNM) by Blackstone Infrastructure. This authorization follows FERC's review, which deemed the transaction to be in line with public interest. No specific numbers were provided in the news snippet.

data.title: Stocks rise after Supreme Court rules against Trump's global tariffs. Nasdaq leads higher; Nvidia shares up.
data.description: Stocks experienced a positive turn today following the Supreme Court's decision against President Trump's global tariffs. The Nasdaq surged after the announcement, with Nvidia shares also experiencing an increase. This news has provided a boost to the market, indicating a favorable trend for investors.

data.title: FDA approves BYSANTI™ (milsaperidone) by Vanda Pharmaceuticals for Bipolar I Disorder and Schizophrenia. New therapy offers innovative treatment options.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, Vanda Pharmaceuticals Inc. revealed that BYSANTI™ (milsaperidone) tablets have been officially approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). Trading under Nasdaq symbol VNDA, this medication has been recognized as a primary treatment option for managing manic or mixed episodes linked to certain conditions. This approval marks a significant milestone for Vanda Pharmaceuticals Inc. and potentially offers an essential solution for individuals dealing with these types of episodes.

data.title: SmartStop Self Storage REIT in Ladera Ranch, California appoints a new management board member.
data.description: LADERA RANCH, California - SmartStop Self Storage REIT has recently added a new member to its management board. This strategic move is aimed at bolstering the company's existing team and enhancing its operational effectiveness. The addition of this new member is expected to bring valuable expertise and fresh perspectives to the board. This development comes as SmartStop Self Storage REIT continues to expand its presence in the self-storage industry.
US TREASURY SECRETARY BESSENT SAYS IF THERE IS A PAYOUT, IT LOOKS LIKE CORPORATE WELFARE.

data.title: Institutional Property Advisors brokers sale and financing for Luxe Villas, a 60-unit Los Angeles property sold for $49.5M.
data.description: In Los Angeles, Institutional Property Advisors (IPA), a division of Marcus & Millichap (NYSE: MMI), revealed the successful transaction and funding for Luxe Villas, a 60-unit multifamily property in the Brentwood area. The asset was sold for $49,494,000, equivalent to $824,900 per unit. Kevin Green, IPA's executive managing director, highlighted Luxe Villas as a top-tier multifamily asset in Brentwood.

data.title: K2 Capital Acquisition Corp. allows separate trading of 13.8M units starting Feb. 25, 2026; Class A shares symbol "KTWO."
data.description: K2 Capital Acquisition Corp. revealed today that starting from February 25, 2026, holders of the 13,800,000 units from the Company’s initial public offering can opt to separately trade the Class A ordinary shares and rights included in the units. If not separated, these units will continue trading on the NASDAQ Global Market under the symbol “KTWOU.” However, the separated underlying Class A ordinary shares and rights will trade on the NASDAQ under the symbols “KTWO” and “KTWOR,” respectively. To perform this separation, holders of the units must have their brokers get in touch with the Company's transfer agent, VStock Transfer.
